title: Daniel A. Downie
content:
People.ContactEmail ddownie@mccarthy.ca
- University of Alberta
Daniel helps navigate your most complex, high-stakes business transactions and tax disputes.
Daniel is an associate in McCarthy Tétrault’s National Tax Group, based in Calgary. Daniel works with all sizes and sectors of business on not only their most significant transactions, but also their most complex tax disputes.

#### Planning and Transactional Experience
Daniel focuses on domestic and cross-border tax planning, reorganizations and mergers and acquisitions. He also advises clients on matters relating to the taxation of foreign affiliates, partnerships, and trusts, as well as with the application of the Global Minimum Tax Act .
Daniel has advised on the Canadian tax aspects of several significant M&A and capital markets transactions, with a particular emphasis on the oil and gas and mining industries.
Notable transactions include:
- Cenovus Energy Inc. in its $8.6 billion acquisition of MEG Energy Corp.
- Royal Gold Inc. in its US$3.5 billion acquisition of Sandstorm Gold Ltd. and US$196 million acquisition Horizon Copper Corp.
- Korean Air in its joint acquisition with Delta Air Lines of a combined 25% stake in WestJet
- Lithium Americas (Argentina) Corp. in its continuance from Canada into Switzerland
- Cenovus Energy Inc. in its acquisition of Gear Energy Ltd.
- Silvercorp Metals Inc. in its acquisition of Adventus Mining Corporation
- SECURE Waste Infrastructure Corp. in its repurchase of $250 million of common shares from shareholders
- ElectraMeccanica Vehicles Corp., a Nasdaq-listed manufacturer of electric vehicles, in its acquisition by California-based commercial electric truck startup, Xos, Inc.
- A syndicate of underwriters in connection with a $500 million bought deal offering of common shares of Crescent Point Energy Corp.
- A syndicate of dealers in connection with a $1.5 billion medium term note offering by Suncor Energy Inc.

#### Tax Disputes Experience
Daniel’s extensive tax disputes experience includes dealing with the general anti-avoidance rule, transfer pricing, and resource taxation. He assists clients with disputes at the audit and objection stages as well as in litigation before the Tax Court of Canada and the Federal Court of Appeal.
Notable tax disputes and litigation include:
- A global energy company in the successful settlement of an appeal regarding the application of the general anti-avoidance rule to a “bump” transaction
- A Canadian energy company in the successful settlement of an appeal about whether the disposition of an interest in an oilsands project was on account of income or capital
- A global energy company in an appeal about the application of the general anti-avoidance rule arising from the acquisition and sale of resource properties in Canada
- A Canadian energy company in an appeal about the application of the general anti-avoidance rule arising from an income trust conversion
- A Schedule I Bank in an appeal about the availability of scientific research and experimental development tax incentives
- Multinational enterprises in resolving their transfer pricing disputes
Daniel has published several articles on business-critical issues in the Canadian tax landscape, including:
- “Partnerships: Key Tax Considerations for Owner-Manager Advisors”, 2026 Prairie Provinces Tax Conference (Toronto: Canadian Tax Foundation) (co-author and co-presenter)
- “ ExxonMobil: Tax Court of Canada Finds that Feasibility Study Costs are Deductible ”, MT Blog (May 2026)
- “Corporate Migrations: Canadian Tax Considerations in an Uncertain International Landscape”, 2025 Young Practitioner Focus Conference (September 2025) (co-author and co-presenter)
- “ Settlements: Tax Outcomes Every Litigator Needs to Know ”, MT Blog (July 2025)
- “ Prorogation and the Mineral Exploration Tax Credit: A Warning ”, MT Blog (March 2025)
- “ Tax Perspectives: Review of 2024 and 2025 Outlook ”, MT Blog (February 2025)
- “TCC applies “modern” TCP approach to interpret the FAPI services exceptions” , Canadian Tax Focus 15:1 (February 2025)
- “EIFEL: The Relevant Inter-Affiliate Interest Rules”, International Tax Planning (Federated Press), XXVIII(4):2-9 (2024)
- “Select Observations in the New GAAR Landscape”, 2024 Prairie Provinces Tax Conference (Toronto: Canadian Tax Foundation) (co-author and co-presenter)
- “ Clean Economy Tax Credits: Labour Requirements, as updated by Bill C-59 ”, MT Blog (April 2024)
- “Pinnacle International: Reasonableness under Section 103” , Canadian Tax Focus 14:1 (February 2024) (co-author)
- “ Tax Perspectives: Review of 2023 & 2024 Outlook ”, MT Blog (February 2024)
- Deans Knight: The Supreme Court of Canada Applies the General Anti-Avoidance Rule to Supplement a Well-Established Specific Anti-Avoidance Provision ”, MT Blog (May 2023)
- “TCC on Retrospective Modification of Tax Positions for Loss Years” , Canadian Tax Focus 11:1 (February 2021) (co-author)
- “Nil Assessments Are Not a “Nothing””, Tax Litigation (Federated Press), XXIII(3):8-11 (2020) (co-author)

#### Speaking Engagements
Daniel is a frequent presenter on current tax issues:
- “Update on Canadian GMTA and Recent OECD Administrative Guidance”, 2026 IFA Canada International Tax Conference (May 2026) (panelist)
- “Current Cases”, Tax Executives Institute luncheon (April 2026) (co-presenter)
- “Tips and Traps: How to Effectively Navigate Canadian Tax Disputes and Litigation”, Tax Executives Institute webinar (March 2026) (co-presenter)
- “Section 116”, Canadian Tax Foundation Annual Conference Workshop (November 2025) (co-presenter)
- “Section 116 in Practice”, McCarthy Tétrault Tax Perspectives (September 2025) (co-presenter)
- "Looking South: Outbound M&A and Corporate Migration”, Canadian Petroleum Tax Society Annual Conference (June 2025) (co-presenter)
- “Musings on Section 116”, Canadian Petroleum Tax Society (March 2025) (co-presenter)
- “Impact of Pillar Two rules on M&A Transactions”, McCarthy Tétrault Tax Perspectives (January 2025)
- “Current Cases: GAAR Edition”, Canadian Bar Association (Alberta), Taxation Section Presentation (June 2024)
- Guest Lecturer, Basic Tax, Thompson Rivers University Faculty of Law (November 2022)
- “Select Tax Issues in Cryptocurrency Transactions”, Canadian Bar Association (Alberta), Taxation Section Presentation (November 2022)
- “Legislative Update: Draft Tax Proposals”, Canadian Bar Association (Alberta), Taxation Section Presentation (March 2022)

#### Commitment to the Community
Daniel is committed to volunteering in the community. He is the Chair of the Canadian Tax Foundation – Young Practitioners’ Steering Committee (Calgary) and sits on the board of directors for the Land’s End Chambers Music Society of Calgary. He is a past chair of the Executive Committee of the CBA Alberta Taxation Law (South) Section and a past member of the Program Committee for the 2025 CTF Prairie Provinces Conference.

#### Education and Memberships
Daniel was called to the Alberta Bar in 2020 and earned his Juris Doctor (with distinction) from the University of Alberta in 2019. He is a member of the Law Society of Alberta, the Canadian Bar Association, the Canadian Tax Foundation, the Canadian Petroleum Tax Society, and the International Fiscal Association. Daniel has also completed all three years of the CPA Canada In-Depth Tax Program and the CPTS Canadian Energy Taxation Course.
